posts.title: Wardha Student Pratyansha Sahu Honoured at International World-PEC, Thailand
posts.author: Chetana Education Society
posts.excerpt: Pratyansha Santosh Sahu, a student of Chetana Education Society, was felicitated in Thailand at the International Standard World Peace Ethics Contest — a global platform recognising young achievers for excellence in ethics, values, and peace education.
posts.content:
Wardha Student Pratyansha Sahu Honoured at International World-PEC, Thailand

March 2026

Pratyansha Santosh Sahu, a student of Chetana Education Society, was felicitated in Thailand at the International Standard World Peace Ethics Contest (World-PEC) — a global platform recognising young achievers for excellence in ethics, values, and peace education.

Dr. Chetana Sawai, Secretary of CES, and Mayurji Dafale, Director of CES, personally attended the ceremony. Pratyansha's parents travelled to Thailand to witness the moment.

Principal Dr. Sudhir Jinde congratulated Pratyansha for "bringing international recognition to the school," and the teaching staff praised her dedication and discipline.

This recognition reflects what CES has stood for since 1973: giving first-generation learners from rural Wardha the values, confidence, and platform to compete — and win — on a global stage.

[Photo from The Hitavada, Vidarbha Line, March 9, 2026]

Want to support more students like Pratyansha? Partner with CES →

posts.title: CES Signs International MoU with Dhammakaya Foundation, Thailand
posts.author: Chetana Education Society
posts.excerpt: Chetana Education Society has signed a Framework Memorandum of Understanding with the Dhammakaya Foundation, Thailand — marking the beginning of a new collaborative partnership for education and community development programmes in India.
posts.content:
CES Signs International MoU with Dhammakaya Foundation, Thailand

April 2026

Chetana Education Society has signed a Framework Memorandum of Understanding with the Dhammakaya Foundation, Thailand, through its Indian branch, Dhammacakka Foundation Trust — marking the beginning of a new collaborative partnership to undertake education and community development programmes in India.

The MoU was signed on 23 April 2026 in Thailand by Dr. Chetana Sawai, Secretary of CES, and Bhante Anusorn (Phramaha Anusorn Wongtun), President of Dhammacakka Foundation Trust — a meeting that reflected the shared values of humility, dedication, and service held by both organisations.

Under this framework, CES and Dhammakaya Foundation will jointly develop programmes in two key areas: educational development and social empowerment, with separate detailed project plans to be prepared for each initiative. The first phase of projects is expected to begin in Maharashtra, with several initiatives for students, teachers, and rural communities currently under discussion.

For CES — which has worked in education and rural development in Wardha since 1973 — this MoU marks a meaningful step toward expanding its reach through international collaboration, building on a year that has already seen its students achieve recognition on the global stage.

CES looks forward to sharing updates as these collaborative projects take shape.
